articles.title: RATE-CUT OPTIMISM FUELS $921M IN GLOBAL CRYPTO FUND INFLOWS
articles.summary: Crypto investment products saw $921 million in weekly inflows, rebounding sharply from the prior week's $513 million outflow. The increase was driven by rate-cut optimism after lower CPI data and strong US demand, with Bitcoin funds adding $931 million, their largest gain since rate cuts began, while Ethereum products saw $169 million in outflows, their first dip in five weeks. Trading volumes held strong at $39 billion, well above average, and inflows were led by the US ($843M) and Germany ($502M).

articles.title: CITI, COINBASE PARTNER TO EXPAND DIGITAL ASSET PAYMENTS
articles.summary: Citi partnered with Coinbase to develop digital asset payment solutions for institutional clients, initially focusing on streamlining fiat on/off-ramps and payment orchestration with future plans for 24/7 fiat-to-stablecoin options. The partnership extends Citi's push into round-the-clock digital money solutions alongside existing offerings like Citi Token Services and USD Clearing across its 94-market network. Citi currently banks 90% of top e-commerce firms and 15 of the world's 20 largest fintechs. It plans to launch crypto custody services in 2026 to hold native digital assets like Bitcoin and Ethereum on behalf of clients.

articles.title: JAPAN'S JPYC LAUNCHES FIRST LEGALLY APPROVED YEN STABLECOIN
articles.summary: JPYC Inc. has launched Japan's first legally recognized yen-backed stablecoin, fully compliant under the country's Payment Services Act. Pegged 1:1 to the yen and backed by bank deposits and government bonds, the JPYC token is live on Avalanche, Ethereum, and Polygon, with issuance and redemption handled via the new JPYC EX platform. Users can acquire tokens after KYC through Japan's My Number ID system. JPYC targets ¥10 trillion ($65B) in circulation within three years. Multiple Japanese firms plan to integrate JPYC into retail and enterprise payment systems, signaling Japan's entry into regulated, large-scale stablecoin adoption.

articles.title: STABLECOIN LINKED CARDS - WAY BETTER SECURED CREDIT CARDS
articles.summary: Stablecoin-linked cards allow users to spend USDC or USDT at any Visa/Mastercard merchant through instant fiat conversion at point of sale. Self-custodial options like Ether.fi allow users to borrow against crypto collateral at 4% APY while earning 10% yield. Revolut's zero-fee stablecoin conversion for 65 million customers and Coinbase's Bitcoin-backed loans powered by onchain protocols like Morpho demonstrate how vaults are bringing decentralized lending to mainstream fintech apps, replacing balance sheet lending with private credit models. The shift toward direct stablecoin acceptance by merchants and banks will compress settlement times to zero, eliminating the 1-2% early payment fees merchants currently pay and the up to 2% consumer on/off ramp costs.

articles.title: WHY X402 MATTERS
articles.summary: x402 transforms how AI agents pay for data and services by replacing traditional payment rails, which are slow, risky, and require human setup, with instant, trustless crypto settlements. Using USDC microtransactions, agents can autonomously authorize and complete payments directly with APIs, removing middlemen and chargebacks. This shift enables fully autonomous AI agents and lets API providers design new onchain business models where data, compute, and services can transact seamlessly in real time.

articles.title: A NEW PATH FOR PROPRIETARY AMMS
articles.summary: Proprietary AMMs managed by professional market makers have captured 40%+ of Solana trading volume by updating pricing curves up to 74 times per second, enabled by extremely cheap updates compared to swaps that allow market makers to achieve Top of Block priority with minimal fees and avoid front-running. EVM's expensive storage writes versus swaps create only a 1:10 ratio instead of Solana's 1:1000, making updates costly and vulnerable to arbitrageur front-running since outbidding requires only 10x the priority fee. A proposed solution combines a Global Storage smart contract for price curve parameters with a builder policy that reserves the first 5-10% of block gas exclusively for these updates ordered by priority fee, creating a protected "fast lane" that guarantees Top of Block execution and establishes a local fee market competing only against other updates rather than all DeFi transactions.

articles.title: LOCKONOMICS
articles.summary: Velodrome and Aerodrome – the leading DEXes on Optimism and Base – pioneered "lockonomics," a model where users lock tokens for up to 4 years to receive voting power, protocol fees, and project bribes, while LPs receive token emissions instead of direct fees. The system imposes a "speculation tax" on short-term holders through inflation that benefits lockers – currently 50% of supply is locked with an average duration of 3.6 years – and can be stabilized via emission control to match revenue when growth slows. Velodrome currently runs 15% annualized inflation while delivering 20%+ APY to lockers, deliberately operating at emissions exceeding revenue to drive sticky growth through accumulated protocol-owned liquidity and user lock-in before eventually reverting to a sustainable emissions-equal-revenue equilibrium.

articles.title: WTF IS ORCHESTRATION (OR CLEARING) (OR SETTLEMENT)
articles.summary: Orchestration (coordinating between parties), clearing (reconciling who owes what), and settlement (actual fund transfers) evolved in traditional finance to coordinate transactions across networks of intermediaries maintaining separate ledgers. Crypto's multi-chain reality has recreated the same coordination challenges that shaped traditional finance, despite blockchain's promise of instant settlement on a single shared ledger. The opportunity lies in rebuilding these functions through smart contracts, cryptographic proofs, and programmable liquidity rather than institutional intermediaries, creating systems that are more performant, upgradeable, and transparent to handle crypto's expected orders-of-magnitude higher velocity of money.

articles.title: POLYGON'S B2B PAYMENT VOLUME MOVES TOWARD $500M
articles.summary: B2B payment projects built on Polygon have seen rapid adoption, with monthly volume rising from $140M in January to nearly $300M in August, rebounding after a brief dip in September.

articles.title: KAST SCALES TOWARD BECOMING THE “GLOBAL CRYPTO/FIAT NEOBANK”
articles.summary: KAST has built 580 product versions in just 484 days.

articles.title: INDIAN COURT BARS WAZIRX FROM USING CUSTOMER XRP TO OFFSET HACK LOSSES
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that cryptocurrency is property.

articles.title: MEGAETH RAISES $50M IN MINUTES AS MEGA SALE SELLS OUT 3X OVER
articles.summary: MegaETH, the Ethereum Layer 2 project, sold out its $50 million MEGA token sale within minutes, ending a planned 72-hour auction early after demand exceeded supply by over 3x.
